beat: Injuries, withdrawals, illness, fatigue, fitness concerns

active_concerns.name: Carlos Alcaraz
active_concerns.issue: right wrist injury, sustained mid-match at Barcelona vs Otto Virtanen; tests showed it was 'slightly more serious than expected'
active_concerns.last_match: 2026-04-15 Barcelona R1 vs Virtanen (won, but required treatment, then withdrew before next round)
active_concerns.expected_return: OUT Madrid 2026, OUT Rome 2026, OUT Roland Garros 2026 (defending champion); grass season target
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour, beIN, Outlook India, profootballnetwork: Alcaraz withdrew from Madrid Apr 17, then ruled himself out of Rome and confirmed RG withdrawal Apr 24. Two-time defending RG champ. Loses ~3,000 ranking points.
active_concerns.betting_impact: MASSIVE. Sinner now overwhelming RG favorite (was co-favorite). Outright RG market needs full repricing. Any lingering Alcaraz futures still on the board are dead money. Watch for grass-season odds — wrist injuries on a topspin-heavy player linger; fade Queen's/Wimbledon top-3 lines until match practice.

active_concerns.name: Novak Djokovic
active_concerns.issue: right shoulder injury, originated at Indian Wells
active_concerns.expected_return: OUT Miami, OUT Monte Carlo, OUT Madrid; targeting Rome (May 7) but conceded he may delay until Roland Garros
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour, Tennis.com, Ubitennis, Puntodebreak: Djokovic quote 'I am injured, I hope to at least reach Roland Garros.' Limited match practice on clay heading into RG.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Fade if listed in Rome. Even if he plays Rome it's a comeback rep, not a title run. RG outright at any short price is poor value with no clay matches and a 38yo shoulder. Live-bet against him in early rounds Rome if he enters.

active_concerns.name: Holger Rune
active_concerns.issue: left Achilles tendon — full rupture, surgery October 2025
active_concerns.expected_return: Hamburg ATP 500, May 17-23, 2026 — confirmed by Rune; Roland Garros possible but unlikely match-fit
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour feature, Tennis.com, Puntodebreak: Rune ahead of typical 9-12 month timeline. Steve Johnson publicly called the Hamburg return 'awful' / risky. Training on indoor clay at Mouratoglou.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Comeback from Achilles rupture on clay is the single highest re-injury risk profile in tennis. Fade Rune in Hamburg and any RG outright. First-round opponents (any decent claycourter) are live underdogs. Movement-restricted player on slow clay = drained legs by set 3.

active_concerns.name: Jack Draper
active_concerns.issue: knee tendon injury (patellar/quad tendon area)
active_concerns.first_reported: 2026-04 Barcelona R1 (RTD vs Etcheverry, down 1-4 in 3rd set)
active_concerns.expected_return: OUT Madrid, OUT Rome; 'hopeful' for Roland Garros but unseeded. Defending Madrid finalist + Rome QF — drops 850 pts, projected outside top 50
active_concerns.source_summary: Sky Sports, Tennishead, Tennis365, Yardbarker, Radio Jackie: Draper personally confirmed knee tendon issue, called it 'frustrating'.
active_concerns.betting_impact: If he enters RG unseeded, he's a landmine for top-8 seeds in early rounds — but his own form and fitness are unproven. Draper to win RG match wagers should be priced like a returning player, not a top-7 — value on his opponent in R1 if drawn against a clay-comfortable mid-tier (Tabilo, Cerundolo type).

active_concerns.name: Taylor Fritz
active_concerns.issue: knee tendonitis, ongoing since early 2026
active_concerns.expected_return: OUT Monte Carlo, OUT Munich, OUT Madrid; Rome status unclear but trending negative; RG questionable
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennishead, Tennisuptodate: Fritz cited continued knee tendonitis recovery in Madrid withdrawal statement.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Fritz on clay is already a fade; injured Fritz on clay is a hard fade. If he enters Rome rusty, opening-round opponent is live. RG futures at any meaningful price are poor — he hasn't played a clay match in 2026.

active_concerns.name: Arthur Fils
active_concerns.issue: lower back stress fracture (recurrence/management); withdrew Monte Carlo
active_concerns.first_reported: Stress fracture initially May 2025; recurrence forced Monte Carlo 2026 withdrawal
active_concerns.expected_return: Status fluid; reached Doha SF, Indian Wells QF, Miami SF, Qatar F earlier in 2026 — body holding then breaking down again
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour, Yahoo Sports, Tennis Gazette: 'I can't tell myself I will never have back pain again.' Withdrew Monte Carlo as 5th highly-ranked withdrawal.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Back issues + heavy clay grinding = serial fade in 3-set situations. If he plays Rome/RG, take opponent in any match scheduled day-after-day. Live unders on his match games when he's serving second set.

active_concerns.name: Jakub Mensik
active_concerns.issue: toe injury (delayed clay start) — appears managed
active_concerns.expected_return: Active in Madrid; played deep into draw; likely Rome/RG full participation
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour Madrid recap; Mensik 'looked solid' despite toe — Miami champion.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Probably mostly priced in. But 20yo with a toe issue grinding through clay = monitor for set-3 fade angles in best-of-5 RG.

active_concerns.name: Sebastian Korda
active_concerns.issue: undisclosed; withdrew Monte Carlo and Madrid
active_concerns.expected_return: Unclear; missed both 1000s of clay swing
active_concerns.source_summary: SI, Tennis Tour Calendar withdrawal lists.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Treat as untested entering Rome/RG. Fade in any short-price spot.

active_concerns.name: Frances Tiafoe
active_concerns.issue: withdrew from Madrid (reason not specified in pulled sources)
active_concerns.source_summary: ATP Tour Madrid coverage notes Tiafoe and Korda withdrew, opening bracket.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Tiafoe already an inconsistent claycourter; layoff worsens it. Fade vs anyone with clay reps.

active_concerns.name: Iga Swiatek
active_concerns.issue: viral illness — dizziness, blurred vision, 'zero energy, zero stability'
active_concerns.last_match: 2026-04-26 Madrid R3 RTD vs Ann Li, trailing 6-7(4) 6-2 0-3
active_concerns.expected_return: Rome (May 6) likely if virus clears; RG full participation expected
active_concerns.source_summary: WTA, Ubitennis, Tennisuptodate, Tennis365: Swiatek tearful, doctor checked vitals on court. Multi-day virus.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Highest-edge spot if she enters Rome early. Markets price her at near-favorite on clay regardless. If she's listed in Rome R1 within 7 days of Madrid retirement, opponent is value — virus recovery + clay grinding + best-of-3 don't recover that fast. Watch her practice reports out of Rome.

active_concerns.name: Madison Keys
active_concerns.issue: illness (unspecified)
active_concerns.source_summary: WTA, Justwomenssports, Tennishead: pulled out hours before opener; replaced by Anastasia Potapova.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Insufficient info; treat any Rome match as live opponent value if she enters within a week.

active_concerns.name: Clara Tauson
active_concerns.issue: shoulder injury
active_concerns.last_match: Withdrew before Madrid opener vs Siniakova
active_concerns.expected_return: Unclear; shoulder issues on a big-serving player are slow
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennis365, WTA: Tauson was world No.18 seed, replaced by Blinkova.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Fade serve-reliant Tauson into Rome/RG until she plays a tune-up match. Her serve % is everything; injured shoulder = first-serve drop = under-priced opponent on serve-hold markets.

active_concerns.name: Amanda Anisimova
active_concerns.issue: injury (also pulled out of Charleston in same window)
active_concerns.source_summary: WTA, Tennistemple: seeded withdrawal from Madrid alongside Alexandrova.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Top-10 player skipping a 1000 = fitness flag. If listed in Rome short-priced, fade until she shows movement.

active_concerns.name: Ekaterina Alexandrova
active_concerns.issue: injury (unspecified)
active_concerns.source_summary: WTA Madrid withdrawal notice.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Standard returning-from-injury fade until she plays a match.

active_concerns.name: Liudmila Samsonova
active_concerns.issue: illness
active_concerns.last_match: Withdrew before Madrid R3 vs Linda Noskova
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennis365: 30th withdrawal of Madrid event.
active_concerns.betting_impact: If she plays Rome, fade in opener — illness recovery + clay = poor prep.

active_concerns.name: Karolina Muchova
active_concerns.issue: workload management post-Stuttgart final + lingering injury history (wrist/forearm previously)
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennis.com, Tennisuptodate: cited illness in some reports, workload in others. Body of work suggests both.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Muchova always one match from a withdrawal. Live-betting opponent in 3rd set is the historical edge.

active_concerns.name: Emma Raducanu
active_concerns.issue: viral illness (lingering since February Middle East swing)
active_concerns.source_summary: EssentiallySports, Tennis365: hasn't played since IW; missed entire clay swing through Madrid.
active_concerns.betting_impact: RG opener is a coin-flip-or-worse for opponents at any price; she'll be a sentimental favorite but priced wrong. Hammer the opponent in any first-round RG match if Raducanu is the public side.

active_concerns.name: Barbora Krejcikova
active_concerns.issue: left thigh injury (since Indian Wells)
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennistemple, Madrid withdrawal coverage.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Fade hard. Movement-dependent player with chronic body issues, no clay reps.

active_concerns.name: Marketa Vondrousova
active_concerns.issue: shoulder (since January, 4th consecutive WTA 1000 missed)
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennistemple Madrid withdrawal article.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Lefty serve depends on shoulder; if she returns, fade until match-tested. Opponent moneyline value.

active_concerns.name: Emma Navarro
active_concerns.issue: ongoing illness
active_concerns.source_summary: Tennistemple, EssentiallySports.
active_concerns.betting_impact: Standard illness-return fade.

active_concerns.name: Coco Gauff
active_concerns.issue: stomach illness mid-Madrid — vomited courtside vs Cirstea, then nearly lost to Noskova next round
active_concerns.source_summary: WTA, profootballnetwork: 'I was trying not to throw up on court.' Recovered to win that match but Noskova R16 was a near-loss.
active_concerns.betting_impact: INFERRED: Gauff's level dropped noticeably across Madrid. If she plays Rome immediately after, fatigue/illness carryover is real. Underpriced opponents in early Rome rounds. Live-bet against in second set if she drops first.

recent_marathons.name: Aryna Sabalenka
recent_marathons.match: Madrid R16 vs Naomi Osaka — survived from a set and break down
recent_marathons.fatigue_risk: MODERATE — Sabalenka playing back-to-back days into QF/SF; Osaka match featured a 7-deuce game at 3-3, suggesting heavy physical exchange. Inferred fatigue carryover.

recent_marathons.name: Naomi Osaka
recent_marathons.match: Madrid R16 vs Sabalenka (loss after leading set + break)

recent_marathons.name: Mirra Andreeva
recent_marathons.match: Madrid R16 vs Bondar — won deciding TB after blowing 5-1 third-set lead; then QF vs Fernandez also went the distance
recent_marathons.fatigue_risk: HIGH — back-to-back three-setters into Madrid SF. 18yo with heavy minutes accumulated. Fade in SF/F if listed short.

recent_marathons.name: Coco Gauff
recent_marathons.match: Madrid R32 vs Cirstea (illness, 4-6 7-5 6-1) and R16 vs Noskova (escape)
recent_marathons.fatigue_risk: HIGH — illness + two grueling 3-setters. Carryover into Rome very likely.

recent_marathons.name: Elena Rybakina
recent_marathons.fatigue_risk: MODERATE — then upset by Potapova in midnight QF, suggesting fatigue from Zheng match was real

extended_absences_returning.name: Emma Raducanu
extended_absences_returning.absence: March 2026 - present (~7 weeks; full clay swing missed)

tournament_status_summary.madrid_2026_withdrawals_total: 31 across both tours (per Tennis365 tally Apr 26-27)

notes: Sources: ATP Tour, WTA Tour, Tennis.com, Tennis365, Tennisuptodate, Ubitennis, Puntodebreak, Tennistemple, ProFootballNetwork, EssentiallySports, Tennishead, Yardbarker, beIN, SI, Sky Sports.
Inferred items flagged in betting_impact text. Confirmed reporting otherwise.
Madrid 2026 still in progress as of 2026-04-29 (QF/SF stage).
